def_zh: 与乌托邦相反的想象社会：以《1984》《美丽新世界》《华氏451》《使女的故事》《饥饿游戏》为代表的文学类型，用极端化的未来警示当下的政治与技术趋势。
def_en: An imagined society worse than our own — the inverse of utopia. The canonical quartet (Orwell, Huxley, Bradbury, Atwood) supplies modern English with its vocabulary for warning about surveillance, hedonism, censorship and patriarchy.

sources.chapter: 1984 / Brave New World / Fahrenheit 451 / The Handmaid's Tale

etymology: dys-（希腊语“坏、恶”）+ topos（地方）——莫尔造 utopia（乌有之乡）三百年后，密尔1868年在议会演讲中造出其反义词。

synonyms.sentence: Every dystopia is a cautionary tale with a plot.
An anti-utopia takes a promise and follows it to prison.

example: Critics of the social-credit experiments reached, inevitably, for the dystopian shelf: part Orwell, part Huxley.
nuance: 奥威尔式与赫胥黎式是两种相反的反乌托邦：前者靠恐惧与匮乏统治（老大哥在看着你），后者靠快感与丰裕统治（无人想读被禁的书，因为无人想读书）。《经济学人》引用时常暗含这一区分。

def_zh: 源自《1984》的一组英语政治词汇：Big Brother（老大哥＝无所不在的监控者）、doublethink（双重思想）、newspeak（新话＝为限制思想而设计的语言）、memory hole（记忆洞）、thought police（思想警察）。
def_en: The vocabulary Nineteen Eighty-Four donated to politics: Big Brother, doublethink, newspeak, the memory hole, thought police. 'Orwellian' describes state deception and surveillance — the highest compliment English pays a novelist is an adjective.
etymology: Orwell 为埃里克·布莱尔的笔名，取自英格兰 Orwell 河；-ian 使人名成形容词——与 Kafkaesque、Dickensian 同属“作家变形容词”俱乐部。

synonyms.sentence: A surveillance state files what it cannot yet punish.
Doublethink lets an official believe the statistics he falsified.

example: Renaming the ministry's censorship office a 'truth and harmony bureau' was an Orwellian flourish the columnists could not resist.
nuance: Orwellian 描述的是被批评的体制，而非奥威尔本人的风格；夸奖某人文笔像奥威尔要说 Orwell-like clarity。此外老大哥真人秀（Big Brother）已使该词在流行文化中软化，政治语境仍取原义。

def_zh: 奥威尔以农场动物影射苏联革命史的寓言：起义（十月革命）、拿破仑猪（斯大林）、雪球（托洛茨基）、七诫的篡改——名句“所有动物一律平等，但有些动物比其他动物更平等”成为特权的英语代号。
def_en: Orwell's barnyard retelling of Soviet history. 'All animals are equal, but some animals are more equal than others' is now English's standard sarcasm for privileged equality; 'Napoleon the pig' needs no footnote.
etymology: allegory 源自希腊 allos（他）+ agoreuein（公开说）——“说此指彼”；动物寓言（beast fable）传统上溯伊索。

synonyms.sentence: Satire is anger that learned table manners.
A fable convicts the reader before naming the defendant.

example: When the co-op's founders voted themselves salaries, someone left a copy of Animal Farm in the break room.
nuance: 读过中国史教材的读者占优势：这部寓言逐段对应苏联史（新经济政策、集体化、大清洗、德苏条约），映射越熟悉,讽刺越锋利。中文世界常译“动物农场／动物庄园”两名并行。

en: Brave New World: the soft dystopia
def_zh: 赫胥黎1932年的反乌托邦：以基因分级、条件反射教育与快乐药“唆麻”（soma）维持的极权——不靠恐惧靠快感。书名反讽地取自莎士比亚《暴风雨》台词。
def_en: Huxley's dystopia of engineered castes, conditioning and the happiness drug soma — tyranny by pleasure rather than pain. The title is Miranda's line from The Tempest, used with maximum irony.
etymology: soma 借自梵语（吠陀祭祀的神饮）兼希腊语 sōma（身体）——一词双关：神性的麻醉，身体的统治。

synonyms.sentence: State hedonism sedates the citizens it no longer needs to convince.
Conditioning manufactures consent before the age of consent.

example: Infinite-scroll entertainment looks less like Big Brother and more like soma — a brave new world we swiped into voluntarily.
nuance: 英语中 a brave new world of X 常半反讽地指“令人不安的新时代”（a brave new world of gene editing）；直译“勇敢的新世界”丢失反讽。与《1984》的对照（快感 vs 恐惧）是英文评论的常用框架。

def_zh: 海勒同名小说贡献给英语的普通名词：自相矛盾、无法逃脱的规则困境——想以疯狂为由停飞就证明你清醒，清醒就必须飞。今泛指一切循环悖论式困境。
def_en: From Heller's novel: a self-defeating rule you cannot escape — pleading insanity to stop flying proves you sane enough to fly. Now the standard English noun for any circular bind.
etymology: catch 此处取“圈套、暗藏的难处”义（What's the catch?）；数字22无义——初稿本为 Catch-18，因撞书名改之，偶然性本身很海勒。

synonyms.sentence: A double bind punishes both obedience and refusal.
No experience, no job; no job, no experience — the oldest vicious circle.

example: Start-ups face a catch-22: banks lend only against revenue, and revenue needs the loan.
nuance: 已完全普通名词化,不需斜体或解释,常小写；中文语境须整句意译（“先有鸡还是先有蛋”只覆盖部分场景，catch-22 强调规则本身的恶意设计）。

def_zh: 源自塞万提斯《堂吉诃德》（1605，常被称为第一部现代小说）：quixotic＝理想主义到不切实际的；tilt at windmills（挑战风车）＝与想象出来的敌人作战。
def_en: From Cervantes' Don Quixote, often called the first modern novel: quixotic means noble-mindedly impractical; to tilt at windmills is to battle imaginary enemies. Both are Economist staples.
etymology: 西语 Quixote 读“基霍特”，英语却按拼写读 /kwɪk-/——词形入英后本土化发音，与人名原音分家，本身就是一堂借词课。

synonyms.sentence: Idealistic plans budget for everything except reality.
The crusading auditor charged at every windmill in the ledger.

example: Analysts called the one-man lawsuit against the cartel quixotic — right up until the windmill fell over.
nuance: quixotic 兼含敬意与讥讽：既赞其理想主义,又断其必败——比 impractical 温柔,比 heroic 悲观。注意发音 /kwɪkˈsɒtɪk/,而人名 Don Quixote 今多读西语式 /ˌdɒn kiːˈhəʊti/。

en: The Great Gatsby & the American Dream
def_zh: 菲茨杰拉德1925年小说：白手起家的盖茨比、绿灯（可望不可即的欲望对象）、爵士时代（Jazz Age，菲氏自造词）——英语中讨论美国梦及其幻灭的第一引用源。
def_en: Fitzgerald's 1925 novel is English's first citation for the American Dream and its hollowness: Gatsby the self-made striver, the green light of unreachable desire, the Jazz Age (Fitzgerald's own coinage).
etymology: American Dream 一词1931年由历史学家 James Truslow Adams 定名——小说先于口号六年写出其挽歌。

synonyms.sentence: The self-made man edits the chapters about his investors.
The Great Gatsby Curve plots social mobility against inequality — economists borrowed the title knowingly.

example: Economists christened the inequality-mobility chart the Great Gatsby Curve — proof the novel now footnotes the discipline.
nuance: 经济学正式借用：Great Gatsby Curve（克鲁格2012）指不平等越高、代际流动越低——文学典故变成计量图表的名字。old money／new money（世家旧富与暴发新贵）之分也由此书普及。

def_zh: 玛丽·雪莱1818年小说（常被称为第一部科幻小说）：科学家造物反噬其主。今为讨论人工智能、基因编辑等技术风险的首选英语隐喻；亦贡献 Frankenfood（转基因食品贬称）等构词。
def_en: Mary Shelley's 1818 novel — arguably the first science fiction — gave English its master metaphor for creations that turn on their creators, from AI to GMOs (Frankenfood).
etymology: Franken- 已成活跃前缀（Frankenfood, Frankenfish）——一个人名裂解为构词语素，是文学影响语言的极端案例。

synonyms.sentence: Every technology writes its own creation myth, then meets its monster.
Unintended consequences are the monster's legal name.

example: Regulators worry the model's creators have built a Frankenstein they can neither explain nor unplug.
nuance: 常见误用：Frankenstein 是科学家的名字，怪物无名——严格说应说 Frankenstein's monster,但日常英语已普遍用 Frankenstein 指怪物本身，《经济学人》两用皆见。副标题 The Modern Prometheus 连通希腊盗火神话。

def_zh: 形容词化的狄更斯（《双城记》《远大前程》）：指维多利亚式的贫困、童工、济贫院与官僚荒诞。名句“这是最好的时代，也是最坏的时代”是英语评论最常引的开头；Scrooge（守财奴）也出自狄更斯。
def_en: Dickens as adjective: Victorian squalor, child labour, workhouses and bureaucratic absurdity. 'It was the best of times, it was the worst of times' is journalism's most borrowed opening; Scrooge is its byword for misers.

sources.chapter: A Tale of Two Cities / Great Expectations (Dickens)

etymology: Dickens + -ian——与 Orwellian 同式；Scrooge、Micawber、Podsnappery 等角色名皆已入词典：狄更斯是为英语造人名普通名词最多的作家。

synonyms.sentence: Dickensian squalor persists wherever the inspector's route does not.
The workhouse fed the poor just badly enough to keep poverty unpopular.

collocations: the best of times, the worst of times
example: Reports described Dickensian conditions in the warehouse — a tale of two economies inside one postcode.
nuance: a tale of two X 已是英语标题万能模板（A Tale of Two Chinas／Recoveries／Housing Markets）——原书写法国大革命,今用其对照结构而非情节。狄更斯与教材工业革命、城市化单元互为脚注。

en: Lord of the Flies: the thin veneer
def_zh: 戈尔丁1954年小说：流落荒岛的男孩们从文明退化为部落暴力——“文明只是薄薄一层外壳”的标准英语引用；海螺（conch）成为议事规则与合法性的象征。
def_en: Golding's boys descend from choir to tribe: English's standard citation for the thin veneer of civilisation. The conch — speak only while holding it — is shorthand for procedural legitimacy and its fragility.
etymology: 书名直译自希伯来语 Beelzebub（巴力西卜，“苍蝇之王”）——魔鬼别名；小说以昆虫学面目引神学典。

synonyms.sentence: Hobbes's state of nature got its film adaptation on Golding's island.
Mob rule begins when the conch stops being passed.

collocations: a Lord of the Flies situation
example: Deprived of moderators for a week, the forum went full Lord of the Flies by Wednesday.
nuance: 与卢梭“高贵野蛮人”相反的性恶论寓言——2020年真实荒岛少年互助生还的报道（Rutger Bregman）常被引来反驳戈尔丁,两案并引已成英文评论套路。

def_zh: 阿特伍德1985年小说：神权国家基列（Gilead）将女性降为生育工具。使女的红袍白帽已成全球妇女权利抗议的现成符号；阿特伍德坚称其为“推演小说”——书中一切皆有史实先例。
def_en: Atwood's Gilead reduces women to wombs of the state. The handmaid's red cloak is now protest costume worldwide; Atwood calls it speculative fiction because every practice in it has a historical precedent.
etymology: handmaid 为《圣经》旧词（婢女，创世记辟拉代孕典）——阿特伍德整套术语皆刻意仿经文，神权以词汇先行。

synonyms.sentence: A theocracy legislates from a book its subjects may not annotate.
Reproductive rights debates now arrive pre-costumed in red cloaks.

example: Outside the statehouse, rows of red cloaks made the legislature's agenda legible without a single placard.
nuance: 与《1984》同属监控极权，但轴心是性别而非阶级；英文评论区分 Orwellian（政治监控）与 Gileadean（性别神权）。文学符号变街头装束,是文学进入政治的当代顶级案例。

en: To Kill a Mockingbird & Atticus Finch
def_zh: 哈珀·李1960年小说：大萧条时期南方小镇的种族冤案。律师阿蒂克斯·芬奇是英语世界程序正义与职业操守的头号偶像——明知必败仍为黑人被告辩护；“杀死知更鸟”即伤害无辜。
def_en: Harper Lee's Depression-era South: Atticus Finch, defending a doomed Black client because the law deserves it, is the Anglosphere's icon of due process; to kill a mockingbird is to harm the innocent.
etymology: mockingbird（嘲鸫）由 mock（模仿）得名——只模仿他鸟之歌、于人无害，故“杀之是罪”；中译“知更鸟”实为误译（知更鸟是 robin），将错就错已成通名。

synonyms.sentence: Atticus lost the verdict and vindicated due process — both mattered.
Moral courage is doing the arithmetic of defeat and proceeding anyway.

collocations: it's a sin to kill a mockingbird
example: The defence bar still invokes Atticus Finch the way physicists invoke Newton — a founding constant of the profession.
nuance: 美国法学院与律协言必称 Atticus,与宾厄姆《法治》同读效果最佳：一个给出法治的八项原则,一个给出法治的人格化身。中译书名之误（mockingbird≠知更鸟）本身是绝佳翻译课案例。

en: Dostoevsky: crime, conscience and the Grand Inquisitor
def_zh: 《罪与罚》（超人理论者拉斯柯尔尼科夫杀人后被良心审判）与《卡拉马佐夫兄弟》（宗教大法官章：人愿以自由换面包）——英语思想写作引用最频的两台俄国思想实验。
def_en: Crime and Punishment (Raskolnikov's superman theory broken by conscience) and The Brothers Karamazov (the Grand Inquisitor: men trade freedom for bread) — the two Russian thought experiments English essayists cite most.

sources.chapter: Crime and Punishment / The Brothers Karamazov (Dostoevsky)

etymology: 英译名句 If God does not exist, everything is permitted 实为萨特转述的浓缩——原文更迂回；名句常常是翻译的再创作。

synonyms.sentence: Conscience is the court that convenes after the acquittal.
Every paternalist state rehearses the Grand Inquisitor's defence: bread is kinder than freedom.

example: The Grand Inquisitor gets quoted whenever a government offers welfare in exchange for obedience — bread for freedom, the oldest trade.
nuance: “自由换面包”与教材中“生存权发展权是首要人权”的论述构成正面对话——同一问题的两种答案，读《经济学人》中国报道时此典频现。

def_zh: 托翁开篇“幸福的家庭都是相似的，不幸的家庭各有各的不幸”被科学界借为方法论：成功须同时满足全部条件，失败只需一条不满足——贾雷德·戴蒙德用它解释为何仅十四种大型动物被驯化。
def_en: Tolstoy's opening line — happy families are all alike; every unhappy family is unhappy in its own way — became a scientific principle: success requires passing every test, failure needs only one. Diamond uses it to explain why so few animals were ever domesticated.
etymology: 俄语姓氏阴性形式 -ina：Karenin 之妻作 Karenina——英译保留此屈折，是英语读者初遇俄语语法之处。

synonyms.sentence: Each necessary condition is a veto the project must survive.
Every unhappy system fails at its own single point of failure.

example: Venture capital obeys the Anna Karenina principle: successful startups clear every hurdle, failed ones each trip on a different fence.
nuance: 文学名句→科学原理的罕见路径：《枪炮、病菌与钢铁》第九章使其成为生态学与商学院通货。引用时通常只需前半句,后半句读者自动补全——英文名句的“接龙默契”。

def_zh: 以马尔克斯《百年孤独》为旗帜的拉美文学手法：把神迹当日常、把史实当传说地平静叙述。马孔多镇＝拉美史缩影；香蕉公司大屠杀章直写真实历史。莫言获诺奖使该词进入中文批评主流。
def_en: The Latin American mode flying García Márquez's flag: miracles narrated deadpan, history narrated as legend. Macondo compresses a continent's history; the banana-company massacre is real. The Nobel committee cited Mo Yan's 'hallucinatory realism' — the term travelled to China.

sources.chapter: One Hundred Years of Solitude (Gabriel García Márquez)

etymology: 德语 Magischer Realismus 本为1925年艺术批评词，经西语 realismo mágico 转世为文学旗号——术语自己也有一部迁徙史。

synonyms.sentence: The Latin American Boom exported novels the way neighbours exported coffee.
Magical realism is deadpan delivery applied to miracles.

example: Reporting from the flooded delta read like magical realism — which is to say, like accurate Latin American history.
nuance: 易误为“奇幻文学”：魔幻现实主义的魔幻服务于历史创伤的表达（殖民、内战、跨国公司），奇幻则自建世界。马尔克斯自辩：拉美现实本身即魔幻,他只是照实写。

def_zh: 雨果1862年巨著：冉阿让因偷面包服役十九年，出狱后被主教之恩改造；沙威代表无慈悲的法条主义。背景为1832年巴黎六月起义（非大革命）。“偷面包”已成英语讨论罪与贫的速记。
def_en: Hugo's Jean Valjean serves nineteen years for stealing bread and is remade by a bishop's mercy; Javert embodies law without grace. Set against the 1832 June Rebellion (not the Revolution). 'Stealing bread' is now shorthand for poverty-driven crime.
etymology: 法语书名保留不译——misérables 兼指“悲惨者”与“卑贱者”，英语无一词能双关，遂径用法文，缩称 Les Mis。

synonyms.sentence: One act of clemency financed a lifetime of restitution.
Javert's legalism could enforce every statute and understand none.

collocations: stealing bread to feed a family
example: Sentencing reformers reach for Valjean: nineteen years for a loaf is the picture their statistics cannot paint.
nuance: 沙威 vs 主教＝rule by law vs mercy 的文学版，与教材“法治与德治相结合”意外同题。注意历史场景是1832年六月起义,英文评论常误作1789——纠正它是加分项。

def_zh: 《老人与海》作者的写作学说：八分之七沉在水下——只写表面动作，情感靠省略传达。Hemingwayesque＝极简、短句、白描。名句“人可以被毁灭，但不能被打败”。
def_en: From the author of The Old Man and the Sea: show the eighth above water, sink the rest. Hemingwayesque means short declarative sentences and surgical omission. 'A man can be destroyed but not defeated.'

sources.chapter: The Old Man and the Sea (Ernest Hemingway)

etymology: iceberg 借自荷兰语 ijsberg（冰山，berg=山）——理论名取“水下八分之七”之喻，见于《午后之死》。

synonyms.sentence: Understatement trusts the reader to supply the scream.
In the iceberg theory, omission is a load-bearing wall.

example: Editors prescribe Hemingway to florid writers the way doctors prescribe fasting: cut until only the necessary survives.
nuance: 对中文写作者尤具校正意义：中文书面语偏繁复对仗,海明威式英语以省略与短句为力量——学术英语与新闻英语的默认审美更近海明威而非狄更斯。

def_zh: 柯南·道尔塑造的咨询侦探：英语中理性推断的人格化身。名言“排除一切不可能，剩下的再不可思议也是真相”；elementary、no-brainer 式的 Sherlock 反讽（“可真是大侦探啊”）皆入日常英语。
def_en: Conan Doyle's consulting detective personifies inference in English. 'When you have eliminated the impossible, whatever remains must be the truth'; calling someone 'Sherlock' is now standard sarcasm for stating the obvious.

sources.chapter: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes (Conan Doyle)

etymology: 严格说福尔摩斯多用溯因推理（abduction，皮尔士术语：由果推最佳解释）而非演绎（deduction）——小说把术语用“错”了一百多年，逻辑课的经典开场白。

synonyms.sentence: Abduction guesses the hypothesis that would make the clues unsurprising.
Without a smoking gun, the case rests on the dog that did not bark.

example: The auditors worked like Holmes: the fraud showed not in what the ledger said, but in the entries that did not bark.
nuance: the dog that didn't bark（沉默的狗＝缺失的证据本身是证据）是《经济学人》高频典故。名句 Elementary, my dear Watson 原著从未出现——典故考据的又一案例。

en: Alice in Wonderland: the metaphor mine
def_zh: 卡罗尔两部爱丽丝书是英语隐喻的高产矿：down the rabbit hole（越陷越深的探索）、through the looking glass（颠倒世界）、Cheshire cat grin、Mad Hatter、Red Queen race（红皇后竞赛：拼命奔跑才能留在原地）——最后者已是演化生物学与商业竞争的正式术语。
def_en: Carroll's Alice books are English's richest metaphor mine: down the rabbit hole, through the looking glass, Cheshire-cat grins, Mad Hatters, and the Red Queen's race — running flat out to stay in place, now a formal term in evolutionary biology and business strategy.
etymology: 作者本名 Charles Dodgson,牛津数学逻辑学家——书中悖论与语言游戏皆本行；Jabberwocky 的自造词（chortle, galumph）竟入正规词典。

synonyms.sentence: One footnote and three hours later, the rabbit hole had a bibliography.
Antibiotic resistance is the Red Queen effect with a body count.

example: Chip export controls sent both governments through the looking glass: each running the Red Queen's race just to hold its place.
nuance: Red Queen 假说（Van Valen 1973）是文学典故变正式科学术语的又一例,与 Anna Karenina principle 同类。rabbit hole 今多指网络沉迷式钻研,已半脱离原书。

en: Pride and Prejudice & the marriage market
def_zh: 奥斯汀1813年小说：摄政时代乡绅阶层的婚姻经济学——限定继承（entail）、年金与身价（a year 收入报价）驱动情节。开篇“凡有产业的单身汉总要娶位太太，这是一条举世公认的真理”是英语最著名的反讽句式模板。
def_en: Austen's Regency marriage economics — entailed estates, annuities, men quoted at so much a year. 'It is a truth universally acknowledged...' is English's most productive ironic template.
etymology: prejudice 源自拉丁 prae（先）+ judicium（判断）——“预先判断”；书名原拟 First Impressions（第一印象），改名后两个抽象名词各归一位主角。

synonyms.sentence: It is a truth universally acknowledged that any headline in this format is being ironic.
Becker analysed the marriage market with equations; Austen had done it with dialogue.

example: It is a truth universally acknowledged that a central bank in possession of low inflation must be in want of a crisis — the template outlives the novel.
nuance: 经济学家（贝克尔婚姻经济学）与《经济学人》标题作者都在反复挪用此开篇——识别 It is a truth universally acknowledged 变体是读英文评论的入门测试。entail（限定继承）一词也主要靠奥斯汀存活于普通读者词汇中。

def_zh: 马克·吐温1884年小说：白人少年与逃亡奴隶吉姆沿密西西比河漂流。海明威断言“全部现代美国文学源于此书”——它把口语白话变成文学语言；哈克“那好吧，我就下地狱”一句是良心对法律的著名反叛。
def_en: Twain's boy and the runaway slave Jim raft down the Mississippi. Hemingway claimed all modern American literature descends from it — it made the spoken vernacular literary. Huck's 'All right, then, I'll go to hell' is conscience overruling law.

sources.chapter: The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn (Mark Twain)

etymology: vernacular 源自拉丁 verna（家生奴隶）——“土语”本义“奴隶的语言”，用于一部关于奴隶制的白话小说，词源即评论。

synonyms.sentence: Colloquial prose smuggles truth past the reader's defences.
The picaresque novel travels episode by episode, like the river it follows.

example: When the memo dropped its jargon and started talking like a person, the prose finally lit out for the Territory.
nuance: 与新文化运动的白话文革命同构：文言→白话，标准书面英语→美国口语——胡适们做的事,吐温早四十年在英语里做过。该书因方言与种族词汇长居美国“被禁/被删改书目”前列,book banning 议题的常客。
